description:
## The change

With the runtime split (NA.1), the lattice's `BasicBits::NIL` and
`BasicBits::BOOL` axes are inconsistent — they encode "this is the
nil value" / "this is a bool value" using basic-bit machinery, but
the values themselves now live in the atoms axis.

### Switch implementations (public API preserved)

  src/types.rs:
    Descr::nil() — keep signature; impl returns `atom_lit("nil")`.
    Descr::bool_t() — keep signature; impl returns
                       `atom_lit("true") ∪ atom_lit("false")`.

### Remove

  src/types.rs:
    BasicBits::NIL constant (the bit was an aliased view of an
                              atom now).
    BasicBits::BOOL constant.
    Any `BasicBits::NIL` / `BasicBits::BOOL` references in
    is_subtype / list_clause_empty / intersect implementations.

  src/reducer.rs:
    fold_const's `Const::Nil => Descr::nil()` line stays — its
    impl now returns atom_lit("nil").
    fold_const's `Const::True/False => Descr::atom_lit(...)` lines
    are correct; they no longer disagree with `Descr::bool_t()`.

  src/ir_typer.rs:
    `Const::Nil/True/False` Descr mappings stay literally the same
    code (atom_lit). What changes is the surrounding consistency:
    nil and bool literals now have the same axis representation
    everywhere.

### Lattice math

  After NA.2:
    `Descr::nil()` ⊆ `Descr::atom_top()` — yes, structurally
    (nil is just an atom now).
    `Descr::bool_t()` ⊆ `Descr::atom_top()` — yes.
    `Descr::nil()` ∩ `Descr::bool_t()` — empty (different atom
    literals, disjoint within the atoms axis).

  Comparisons against old code:
    Pre-NA.2: `nil()` and `bool_t()` were structurally disjoint
              (different basic bits) and disjoint from atoms axis.
              Lattice arithmetic worked but didn't match the
              runtime's atom-tagged reality.
    Post-NA.2: structurally consistent. `Descr::nil()` literally
               IS `atom_lit("nil")`.

### Goldens

  Spec dumps may show `:nil` / `:true` / `:false` rendered in
  `atom_lit` form rather than as `nil` / `bool`. Re-bless. The
  pretty-print rule for the reserved IDs (NA.3) restores the
  conventional rendering.

## Removes

  - BasicBits::NIL and BasicBits::BOOL axes.
  - Any `bb.contains(NIL)` / `bb.contains(BOOL)` checks in lattice
    operations — replaced by atoms-axis logic that already exists.
  - The inconsistency where `Descr::bool_t() ∩ atom_lit("true")` was
    empty in the lattice but the values were equal at runtime.

## Depends on

NA.1.

## Hazards

  - Subtle lattice math: existing `is_subtype` / `is_empty` code paths
    that special-case the BOOL or NIL basic bits need to handle the
    atom-axis equivalent. Most code already uses the atoms-axis
    machinery generically, so removal should be clean.
  - Pattern narrowing: `Pattern::Nil` → narrow to `Descr::nil()`
    (now atom_lit("nil")). `Pattern::Bool(true)` → narrow to
    `atom_lit("true")`. Code paths in ir_typer that narrow these
    don't need to change syntactically — `Descr::nil()` and
    `Descr::bool_t()` are still the API names, their impls just
    moved axes.
title: fz-NA.2: type system — Descr::nil/bool_t use atoms axis; remove BasicBits::NIL/BOOL
